category_title: Students with high sensory interests and emerging joint attention
minimal_context: Student A is in the school dinner hall interacting with a dog during an initial session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is the dog for our session today.

STUDENT: (Immediately shifts from sensory interests to full attention on the dog, showing expressions of pleasure as it enters the room.)

TUTOR: You can interact with the dog.

STUDENT: (Begins physical exploration of the dog, treating it as a highly exciting, multi-sensory experience and conceptualising it initially as a 'part-object' rather than a whole, living being.)

TUTOR: What are you doing?

STUDENT: (Continues the physical exploration of the dog, leaning in to explore the dog beginning with his mouth and tongue.)

options.A: Maintain a ratio of praise to correction at 3:1 or 4:1 during the sessions with the dog.
options.B: Make regulative practices explicit so the student knows exactly what to do to demonstrate competency and compliance.
options.C: Allow the student to choose how to structure and use the sessions with the dog.
options.D: Pre-plan specific responses to potential non-compliance during the sessions with the dog.
